Canaan was a small area; as it is described here, it is about 160 miles long and about 50 miles wide. This was the country promised to the father of faith, and the possession of Israel’s descendants. This was that small piece of land where God was known for many ages. It was the Lord's vineyard, a garden enclosed; but like gardens and vineyards, the limited space was made up by the richness of the soil. Though the earth is the Lord's, and all it contains, few know him and serve him; but those few are happy because they are fruitful to God. Also, see how little of the world God gives to his people. Those who have their portion in heaven should be content with a small amount of this earth. Yet a little that a righteous person has, having it from God’s love and with his blessing, is far better and more comfortable than the riches of many wicked.

God here directs men to divide the land among them. They must feel so certain of victory and success while God fights for them that the people who should be entrusted with dividing the land are named.
